title: Chuck Freilich on Office Hours
summary: Chuck Freilich, former Israeli Deputy National Security Adviser, Belfer Center Senior Fellow, and Author of “Israeli National Security: A New Strategy for an Era of Change,” talks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about Israel’s goals for the future, its relationship with the U.S., and his personal experience making aliyah as a teenager.
body: Gallery 1 of 4 Chuck Freilich: Israeli National Security 2 of 4 Chuck Freilich: Israel's National Goals 3 of 4 Chuck Freilich: Is Israel Concerned by U.S. Retrenchment? 4 of 4 Chuck Freilich: Making Aliyah Subscribe to the Office Hours Podcast iTunes | Google Play | Stitcher | Pocket Cast | TuneIn The latest from Belfer See more Chuck Freilich on Office Hours Print Open Section Navigation In this article Close Section Navigation International Security & Defense International Security Program Middle East & North Africa Related Chuck Freilich More From This Expert Israel May Stand Alone vs Iran Israel's National Security Policy Up Next Photo Credit Toggle Associated Press - Virginia Mayo Explainer NATO’s Article 5 Explained: How Collective Defense Works and When It’s Triggered Feb. 24, 2026 by Madeline Sawyer From Defense, Emerging Technology, and Strategy Photo Credit Toggle MSC Event Summary Rupture: (Re)thinking Middle Powers in a Fragmented World Feb. 14, 2026 Photo Credit Toggle MSC/Axel Heimken Event Summary Mushrooming: Tackling Growing Nuclear Proliferation Risk Feb. 14, 2026

_list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The article features an interview with Chuck Freilich discussing Israeli national security. It covers Israel's core national goals, which include ensuring the state's survival, maintaining a Jewish and democratic character, and achieving peace and regional integration. A significant portion of the discussion addresses Israel's potential concerns regarding perceived U.S. retrenchment from the Middle East and the implications for its strategic posture. The concept of 'Making Aliyah' (Jewish immigration to Israel) is also presented as a component of national policy. The context is set within broader international security and defense topics, linking to issues like Iran's regional influence and nuclear proliferation risks.
result.main_content_zh: 本文是对查克·弗莱利奇的访谈，重点探讨以色列的国家安全。内容涉及以色列的核心国家目标，包括确保国家生存、维持犹太与民主属性、实现和平与区域融合。讨论的一个重要方面是以色列对美国可能从中东收缩的担忧，以及这对以色列战略态势的影响。文中也将“回归法”（犹太人移居以色列）作为国家政策的一部分进行阐述。整个讨论被置于国际安全与防御的更广泛议题背景下，关联到伊朗的地区影响力、核扩散风险等安全问题。
